phrasal_verbs.example: He showed up two hours late.

If you keep working like that, you'll burn out.
She didn't pick up when I called.
We've run out of coffee. SOS.
Let's set up a meeting for Friday.

The noise was wearing me down.
The meeting dragged on for hours.
Problems keep piling up.

We need to get to the bottom of this.
Let's wrap up the project by Friday.
Let's hold off on the launch until we get approval.
We're falling behind schedule.

I'll get back to you with the numbers.

Let's go over the contract together.

I back up this idea. / Back up your files!

She's always badmouthing her colleagues.

New cafés keep springing up in this area.
The team sprang into action immediately.

You have to draw the line somewhere.

The discussion fizzled out without a decision.
I'll polish off the report by tonight.

She brushed it off and moved on.
I'll look into the issue and get back to you.
It all boils down to communication.
I'll get around to it this weekend.
How do you account for the missing data?
She laid out the strategy clearly.
The team carried out extensive research.
She set out to change the system.
He finally coughed up the money he owed.
She managed to winkle out the truth from him.
She flounced off without saying goodbye.
Don't bottle up your feelings.
He glossed over the problems in his report.
The conversation veered off topic.
A short walk always peps me up.
Burnout can creep up on you.
She started to spiral down after the breakup.
The news knocked the wind out of me.
He's recovering, but not out of the woods yet.
Everyone was fawning over the new boss.
I totally zoned out during the lecture.
Don't pad out your essay with filler.
Stop fiddling with your phone.
The candle gives off a warm light.
The manager stepped in to resolve the conflict.
He went off on a tangent about his childhood.

idioms.example: Why is he late? — Beats me.
You won $1000? Are you pulling my leg?
You look tired. Take a breather.
You think I'll do it for free? You wish!
Save your breath — he won't listen anyway.
We're short on time — all hands on deck!
I've worked hard for this. I don't need handouts.

idioms.meaning_ru: ты меня подвесил / пропал без ответа
idioms.example: You said you'd explain — but you left me hanging.
No other options — I may have to take my chances.
Sit down. We're not through here.
Your guess was bang-on!
The metaphor was a bit on-the-nose.
Here's a sneaky peek of what we're working on.

idioms.phrase: just giving you a hard time
idioms.example: Relax, I'm just giving you a hard time.
Try to keep your cool, even if things go wrong.
Don't worry, you'll get the hang of it soon.
The numbers don't add up — something's wrong.
She explained it twice — he completely missed the point.
Let's not open that can of worms right now.
He apologised but she just brushed it off.
That comment was out of line — apologise.
You're blowing this out of proportion again.
His message really lifted my spirits.
She gave him the cold shoulder after the argument.
Monday 7am meeting? Hard pass.
I get serious sunday scaries before a big week.
I'm low-key excited about this.
This café is really a vibe.
The new schedule is different but I'm not mad about it.

idioms.phrase: I see where you're coming from, but…
idioms.example: I see where you're coming from, but I disagree.

idioms.phrase: there's a lot to unpack here
idioms.example: There's a lot to unpack here — where do we start?

idioms.phrase: I'm on the fence about this
idioms.example: I'm on the fence about the new policy.
If you push too hard, that might backfire.

idioms.phrase: not a hill I'm willing to die on
idioms.meaning_ru: не тот вопрос, за который готов биться
idioms.example: I disagree, but it's not a hill I'm willing to die on.
You forgot? No biggie!
Oops, my bad — wrong file.
More coffee? — I'm good, thanks.
Road trip? — I'm down!
Did you enjoy it? — Not really.
How's life? — Can't complain.
He was late again. — That figures.
I told you it was hot! — Serves you right.
I gave you extra time — don't push it.
It's risky — I'm just saying.
It's not my style, but you do you.
$40 for that? Sounds about right.
Interview tomorrow — fingers crossed!
Pen or pencil — whatever works.
Fancy a cuppa? I just put the kettle on.
The security's coming — leg it!
I'm gutted — we lost in the final minute.
Stop faffing around and just do it!
He drives a Ferrari — he's minted.
It's only Monday and I'm already knackered.
The project's gone pear-shaped — need your help.
I'm having a mare today — three things went wrong.

grammar_rules.rule: few vs a few vs quite a few
grammar_rules.explanation: few=мало(негатив) | a few=немного(позитив) | quite a few=немало/много. Подсказка: артикль 'a' = есть что-то.
grammar_rules.example: I have few friends (sad). I have a few friends (ok). I have quite a few friends (lots!)

grammar_rules.explanation: После avoid, enjoy, keep, consider, mind → всегда -ing форма (герундий).
grammar_rules.example: He avoids talking about his past. ✅ (NOT: avoids to talk ❌)

grammar_rules.explanation: После 'look forward to' всегда -ing, не инфинитив!
grammar_rules.example: I look forward to SEEING you. ✅ (NOT: to see ❌)

grammar_rules.explanation: После make и let объект + инфинитив БЕЗ to.
grammar_rules.example: She made me rewrite the essay. / Let me help you.

grammar_rules.explanation: need to do (обычный глагол) = needn't do (модальный). Оба верны. needn't = чуть формальнее.
grammar_rules.example: You don't need to worry. = You needn't worry.

grammar_rules.explanation: Не 'должен', а 'ожидается по правилам/договорённости'. Мягче чем must/have to.
grammar_rules.example: You're not supposed to park here. (правило, не личный запрет)

grammar_rules.explanation: must be=почти уверен | might/may/could be=вероятность | can't be=невозможно.
grammar_rules.example: He must be at home. She might be late. He can't be serious.

grammar_rules.explanation: must have done=точно произошло | might have done=возможно | can't have done=точно нет.
grammar_rules.example: She must have forgotten. He might have missed the train.

grammar_rules.explanation: Сожаление о прошлом = Past Perfect после wish/if only. НЕ Past Simple!
grammar_rules.example: I wish I hadn't gone there. ✅ (NOT: I wish I didn't go ❌)

grammar_rules.explanation: После 'it's high time' Past Simple для настоящего с оттенком запоздания.
grammar_rules.example: It's time we started. ✅ (NOT: It's time we start ❌)

grammar_rules.explanation: После never/rarely/hardly/little → порядок слов как в вопросе.
grammar_rules.example: Never have I seen such a thing. / Rarely do we think about it.

grammar_rules.explanation: arrive IN city (город), arrive AT specific place (аэропорт, вечеринка). Никогда 'arrive TO'!
grammar_rules.example: We arrived in Moscow. We arrived at the airport.

grammar_rules.explanation: in the car (внутри) | on the train/bus/plane (пассажир, едешь).
grammar_rules.example: I'm on the train. She's in the car.

grammar_rules.explanation: Города/страны без артикля. The + UK/USA/Netherlands (kingdom/states/republic). The + реки/океаны. The + горные цепи.
grammar_rules.example: London, France (no article). The UK, the Thames, the Alps (with THE).

grammar_rules.rule: at school vs at THE school
grammar_rules.explanation: Без артикля = функция (учиться). С 'the' = здание/место.
grammar_rules.example: He's at school (studying). I'm at the school (building, e.g. visiting).

grammar_rules.explanation: affect = глагол 'влиять' (A=Action). effect = существительное 'результат' (E=End result).
grammar_rules.example: The weather affects my mood. The new policy had a positive effect.

grammar_rules.explanation: me too → только на утверждение. me neither / I don't either → на отрицание.
grammar_rules.example: I don't like coffee. — Me neither. ✅ / I like coffee. — Me too. ✅

grammar_rules.explanation: beside = рядом с (next to). besides = кроме того (in addition to).
grammar_rules.example: She sat beside me. Besides, I'm too tired to go out.

grammar_rules.explanation: people = повседневно. persons = официально, в документах/юридических текстах.
grammar_rules.example: Many people came. / Unauthorized persons only.

grammar_rules.explanation: amount = неисчисляемые. number = исчисляемые. quantity = формально (наука/бизнес).
grammar_rules.example: The amount of water. The number of messages. Large quantities of data.

grammar_rules.explanation: После 'there's no point' всегда -ing. Синонимы: it's useless to / not worth it / a waste of time / why bother?
grammar_rules.example: There's no point in worrying. / Why bother calling him?

confusable_words.explanation: sickness=общее плохо/тошнота | illness=серьёзнее | disease=медицинский диагноз | condition=мягко
gauge=прочувствовать | evaluate=оценить качество | appraise=оценить стоимость | estimate=прикинуть | weigh=взвесить за/против

examples.conscious: Are you conscious of the risks?

confusable_words.explanation: beside=рядом с (next to) | besides=кроме того (in addition to)
futile=тщетный, бесполезный (о действии) | pathetic=жалкий (о человеке или усилии)

modern_vocab.example: He's been quiet quitting for months now.
She has impostor syndrome despite being brilliant.
Hustle culture is burning people out.
His side hustle earns more than his main job.

modern_vocab.meaning_ru: туман в голове, снижение ясности мышления
modern_vocab.example: I can't focus today — total brain fog.

modern_vocab.meaning_ru: лежать в кровати весь день без причины
modern_vocab.example: I spent Sunday bed rotting — no regrets.

modern_vocab.meaning_ru: тревога в воскресенье вечером перед работой
modern_vocab.example: I get serious sunday scaries before a big week.
This café is really a vibe.
She walked in with total main character energy.
Stop futzing around and get to work.
After all the hype, the movie was such a letdown.
He slid into my DMs with a random question.
